title: Slayers
content:
{{Top|leg}} [[File:Slayers.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Supreme Overlord Shimrra and his Slayers]] {{Quote|They are more vonduun crab than Yuuzhan Vong.|High Priest Jakan|''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''}} '''Slayers''' were elite [[Yuuzhan Vong]] soldiers, created at the [[29 ABY/Legends|end]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] to protect [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] and fight the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. Their creation was a violation of the Yuuzhan Vong [[Shaping protocol]]s and [[Yuuzhan Vong castes|caste system]], associating them with the heresy secretly encouraged by the Supreme Overlord. In truth, the Slayers originated as [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] that had been [[Cloning/Legends|grown]] from [[tissue]] samples of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and then transformed and altered by the [[shaper caste]]. ==Description== {{Quote|To prove the Force a farce indeed,
Shimrra's will the shapers heed;
Birthing troops of mingled caste
Great Nas Choka they will outlast!|Onimi|}} The Slayers were muscular and dark-skinned [[Gender/Legends|males]] with stocky builds. Clad typically only in traditional [[Clothing/Legends|waist-wraps]], they bore the insignia of [[Yuuzhan Vong castes|caste]] and [[Escalation|elevation]] appropriate to both [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Priest caste|priests]] and [[Warrior caste|warriors]]. Though bearing features generally identifying them with the Yuuzhan Vong, the Slayers were in fact [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] grown from tissue samples taken from [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], and then transformed by the shaper caste.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' They were equipped with a suite of combat [[biot]]s and their skin—the black color of dried Yuuzhan Vong [[Blood/Legends|blood]]—was in fact supple, fast-healing [[Armor/Legends|armor]] bonded to their bodies, and they were equipped with the spiked close-combat implants known as [[Steng's Talons]], as well as new variants of the [[amphistaff]] and [[coufee]]. They also flew a [[Slayer ship|new type]] of [[Yorik-et|coralskipper]], heavy [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] that resembled [[voxyn]] in appearance and were equipped with new weapon systems, better defenses, and [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] capability; at least some of these [[Starship/Legends|ships]] were also outfitted with passenger compartments. ==History== {{Quote|Our taller warriors kept rejecting the implants. The faster metabolic rate of our shorter warriors is better suited to the rapid cellular activity of the implant biots.|Master Shaper Qelah Kwaad|}} [[File:Shimrra_TUF.jpg|thumb|right|Supreme Overlord Shimrra Jamaane ordered the Creation of the Slayers.]] The Slayers were created by Yuuzhan Vong [[Master Shaper]] [[Qelah Kwaad]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Yuuzhan'tar]]—formerly the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of Coruscant—near the end of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. Introduced as "Shimrra's ''[[Jeedai]]''", they were designed to serve as bodyguards for the [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] and as warriors who could match the Jedi in combat. The Slayers were so named in honor of [[Yun-Yammka]], the [[Yun'o|Yuuzhan Vong god]] of war. When the Supreme Overlord presented the Slayers to the Yuuzhan Vong [[Elite (Yuuzhan Vong)|Elite]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Yuuzhan'tar]], their appearance caused a scandal. Some denounced their creation as heresy and a violation of the caste system, and [[Supreme Commander (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Supreme Commander]] [[Chaan]] opined that the Slayers resembled the malformed outcasts known as [[Shamed Ones]]—but once a pair of Slayers managed to kill Chaan and his entire cadre of ten warriors, criticism was quickly silenced. As well as a practical means of securing their own position, this display of warrior skill also acted as a deterrent against challenges to Shimrra's authority within the Yuuzhan Vong, either from the [[Quorealist]] opposition, the [[Jeedai heresy|''Jeedai'' heresy]], or any who were perturbed by decisions like the creation of the Slayers themselves. The Slayers' first operational deployment was on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Caluula/Legends|Caluula]], where they captured a [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] team including the Jedi [[Kyp Durron]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], but most of the Slayers fell sick when the experimental Yuuzhan Vong-targeting pathogen [[Alpha Red (pathogen)|Alpha Red]] caused them to reject their implants. When Luke Skywalker and [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] faced Shimrra in his throne room, they encountered a second group of Slayers which Shimrra had kept close as bodyguards, and the three Jedi defeated them in a brief but vicious battle. One survivor of the Caluula team remained, however—a warrior on whom the effects of Alpha Red had been arrested by the efforts of the [[Shaper Adept]] attached to the mission. During the closing stages of the Alliance's [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|retaking of Coruscant]], he bravely mounted an attack run on the living world of [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] in his coralskipper. However, Zonama Sekot used the Force to fling the ship out into deep space, preventing the attack. After the end of the war, one Slayer was captured by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] faction known as the [[One Sith]] and was [[Sith training|trained]] as a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], adopting the name [[Darth Vua]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-12-tales-of-the-new-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 12: Tales of the New Republic}} ==Behind the scenes== The Slayers first appeared in [[James Luceno]]'s [[2003]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-12-tales-of-the-new-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 12: Tales of the New Republic}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlächter |es=Asesinos (yuuzhan vong) }} [[Category:Soldiers]] [[Category:Yuuzhan Vong military units]]
